title: Siddharth Vishwanath - Academic Website
text:
This is the source code for the personal and academic website of Siddharth Vishwanath, built using Quarto. It is a data-driven website that leverages YAML for content management and EJS for custom templating.



Technologies: Quarto, EJS, YAML, R (renv), CSS/SCSS.
Architecture:

Content: Managed via .qmd files in the root and subdirectories (e.g., talks/).
Data-Driven Pages: research.qmd and teaching.qmd use custom EJS templates to render lists from YAML data files in src/.
Custom Styling: Extensive custom styling is defined in theme/css/. Fonts are managed separately in fonts.css and applied via CSS variables. SCSS overrides are used for Bootstrap component styling in theme.scss.
Output: The website is rendered into the docs/ directory, which is configured for hosting on GitHub Pages.






_quarto.yml: Main configuration file for the Quarto project.
index.qmd: The landing page of the website.
src/: Contains YAML data files for dynamic content:

publications.yml: List of research papers and preprints.
teaching.yml: History of courses taught or assisted.
news.yml: News items for the homepage (if enabled).

theme/: Contains styling and templating files:

ejs/: Custom EJS templates (research.ejs, teaching.ejs).
css/: Custom CSS and SCSS files.

fonts.css: Declares Google Font imports and global CSS variables (--mainFont, --codeFont) for typography.
theme.scss: Quarto SCSS theme overrides (colors, variables).
custom.css: Custom CSS rules for various components.
colors.css, styles.css, nutshell.css: Specialized CSS modules.

html/: Custom HTML headers and footers.

talks/: Directory containing individual talk pages, each in its own subdirectory with an index.qmd.
files/: Static files such as CV (PDF) and publication posters/papers.
images/: Profile pictures and other visual assets.
docs/: The generated static website files (do not edit directly).
_extensions/: Quarto extensions like fontawesome, academicons, and nutshell.
renv/, renv.lock, .Rprofile: R environment management files.







Quarto CLI
R (optional, for managing dependencies via renv)





Preview the website:
quarto preview
This will start a local server and open the website in your browser with live reloading.
Render the website:
quarto render
This will generate the static files in the docs/ directory.
Restore R environment: If you are using R-based features or extensions:
renv::restore()






Updating Publications/Teaching: Add or edit entries in src/publications.yml or src/teaching.yml. The pages will automatically update upon the next render.
Styling and Typography:

To change global fonts, update the @import rules and CSS variables in theme/css/fonts.css.
To change theme colors or Bootstrap variables, modify theme/css/theme.scss.
For custom layout or component rules, use theme/css/custom.css.

New Talks: Create a new subdirectory in talks/ with an index.qmd file. The talks.qmd listing page will automatically include it.
Freeze Policy: The project uses execute: freeze: true, meaning that computational output is only re-executed when source files change. This is useful for maintaining consistency across builds.
Git Hook: Avoid staging or committing changes in the docs/ directory manually if you have an automated deployment flow, although for simple GitHub Pages setups, docs/ must be tracked.

title: Post With Code
text:
This is a post with executable code.
This chunk executes R code
library(reticulate)
library(JuliaCall)
plot(
    rnorm(100), 
    rnorm(100),
    pch=20,
    col="dodgerblue1"
)
This chunk executes python code
import os
os.getwd()
This chunk executes Julia code

using Plots
plot(
    1:0.1:5, 
    x -> x^1.1 + log(x) * sinpi(x), 
    label=""
)

title: CLAUDE.md
text:
This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.



Preview locally (with live reload): quarto preview
Render to static HTML: quarto render
Render a single file: quarto render research.qmd
Restore R environment (if needed): renv::restore() in R

Output is always written to docs/ — do not edit files there directly.



This is a Quarto-based academic website for Siddharth Vishwanath, deployed to GitHub Pages from the docs/ directory.


Content is split into three layers:

YAML data (src/) — source of truth for publications, teaching, and news
EJS templates (theme/ejs/) — render YAML into HTML fragments consumed by .qmd pages
Quarto pages (root .qmd files) — top-level pages that embed EJS-rendered content

To add a publication, edit src/publications.yml. To add a talk, create talks/<slug>/index.qmd — it will auto-appear on talks.qmd.



Styles are applied in this load order (declared in _quarto.yml): 1. theme/css/colors.css — color variables 2. theme/css/fonts.css — font imports (Google Fonts) and CSS variables (--mainFont, --codeFont, etc.) 3. theme/css/custom.css — component rules 4. theme/css/publications.css — publication list styling 5. theme/css/theme.scss — Bootstrap/Quarto SCSS variable overrides
To change global fonts: edit fonts.css. To change theme colors or Bootstrap variables: edit theme.scss. For layout/component tweaks: use custom.css.



execute: freeze: true is set globally — computational cells only re-run when source files change. This keeps builds fast and reproducible.




_quarto.yml — project config (nav, theme, CSS order, analytics, output dir)
src/publications.yml — all research publications with metadata and links
theme/ejs/research.ejs — EJS template that renders publications list
theme/html/header.html — loads Font Awesome, Academicons, and Juxtapose.js via CDN
